mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% in May 2014 reflects ongoing labor market recovery from 2008-2009 crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic confidence. This mechanism operates through increased capital expenditure, employment multipliers, and consumer wealth effects, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 800 thousand units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 791.0 thousand units in April 2014 near trigger threshold
regime_hint: housing recovery phase in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) when HOUST exceeds trigger threshold, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at historically low levels (near zero) constrains further monetary accommodation and signals the terminal phase of quantitative easing. As the market anticipates policy normalization, equity risk premiums compress and forward returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days due to repricing of duration risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 6 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% in May 2014 reflects zero lower bound constraint
regime_hint: late-stage monetary accommodation with policy normalization expectations
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for extended periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side productivity gains. Rising INDPRO signals robust business investment and operational efficiency, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1173 in May 2014 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 20-day returns show no significant correlation with month-over-month INDPRO growth (correlation < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth and employment gains interact to amplify consumer demand signals. When both RSAFS and PAYEMS rise simultaneously, the joint signal indicates sustained consumption momentum and business hiring confidence, producing stronger positive equity returns over 20 days than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both show month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355655M and PAYEMS at 136167K in May 2014 both in uptrend
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns when both RSAFS and PAYEMS grow > 0.3% are not significantly higher than when only one grows (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% annually signals monetary policy tightening expectations and real interest rate compression. This mechanism operates through expectations of Fed rate increases, which increases equity discount rates and produces negative forward returns over 60 days in a low-rate environment.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.71 in April 2014 reflects moderate inflation
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization expectations with low starting rates
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between inflation and low rates does not produce significantly negative returns compared to high-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 200 thousand per month, signaling robust labor market tightening and rising wage pressures. This mechanism triggers negative equity returns over 5 days as markets reprrice inflation expectations and anticipate earlier Fed tightening in response to tight labor conditions.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 200 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136167K in May 2014 reflects strong hiring momentum
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200K, or the mechanism fails to predict negative short-term returns during periods of accelerating employment.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 interacts with housing starts (HOUST) to signal broad-based demand expansion. When both real GDP growth and housing construction accelerate simultaneously, the joint signal indicates sustained economic momentum and forward-looking business confidence, producing positive equity returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized and HOUST > 750 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11712.3B in Q1 2014 reflects solid growth trajectory
regime_hint: broad-based demand expansion in mid-cycle recovery
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns when both GDPC1 and HOUST signals are positive are not significantly higher than baseline (mean return < 2.0%), or interaction coefficient fails to reach statistical significance at 5% level.
